verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 encodes the type 3 IP3 receptor, a tetrameric intracellular calcium-release channel residing in the endoplasmic reticulum (and to some extent the nuclear envelope). Its calcium-channel pore faces the ER lumen; ligand binding by IP3 occurs on the cytoplasmic face. There is no extracellular domain and no topology that places any portion of the protein body on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. Pre-no checklist: no cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs target ITPR3 on the outer PM face; it is not a sheddable TM precursor; it has no covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the outer leaflet; no documented minority PM pool with outer-leaflet exposure; no gene-specific evidence of cancer/cell-death/activation-induced ecto-display; no germline/tissue-restricted surface display; no lysosomal exocytosis route. The protein is an endomembrane TM channel whose accessible surfaces are cytoplasmic or ER-luminal, not extracellular.
